title: Level 135
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 135: "Nightmarish Sands"}}__NOTOC__{{Quote|Like many levels, '''Level 135''' exists as a space of transition.

Normally, beaches exist between land and sea. On beaches near towns or cities, they may merely serve as a passageway for residents or tourists to reach the ocean for a swim. But here, the beach is all there is, stretching on into infinity and leaving wanderers lost in its expanse.|Unknown wanderer}}{{Class|p1=3|p2=3|p3=0}}
[[File:SandscapeTransparent.png|3000px]]
{{Blurimage|file=Sandscape.jpg|caption=An image of Level 135.|hovertext=Dark sands}} ==Description== '''Level 135''' is a seemingly infinite and mostly flat expanse of sand reminiscent of a beach shore at night; indeed, the sand is somewhat saturated with water as one would expect at a beach. The sky is utterly devoid of stars, a moon, or any other visible celestial body. Nonetheless, one is always provided with an ample and clear view of their surroundings, with the light providing this visibility anomalously emanating from the wanderer's current position. This phenomenon is the only provided light in the level, so one can never observe faraway surroundings while in the level. As one moves throughout the level, one may notice their surroundings become more or less visible. One may end up finding themselves unable to perceive their surroundings a meter away from themselves to being able to see as far as tens of meters into the distance. However, the direction one travels in does not at all seem to dictate how well one can see the level around them, and going back the way one came may not yield the same result as before. If a wanderer in the level were to stay still, their surroundings' visibility would not change at all. {{Blurimage|file=SandscapeChairs2.jpg|caption=Countless beach chairs in Level 135.|hovertext=Beach chairs and umbrellas}} '''Level 135''' has a stark lack of edible or otherwise useful resources, but despite this, various other objects and structures are sparsely located on the level's expanse. All of these aforementioned objects and structures bear some relation to beaches and are things that one would typically expect to find on beaches; these may include sunscreen, beach towels, small beached boats, beach chairs or umbrellas, or even small, abandoned lifeguard towers. Despite the presence of all of these objects, it is impossible to encounter anyone else while in the level; likewise, multiple wanderers entering the level at the same time will be separated upon entry. One's relative position and surroundings in '''Level 135''' seemingly change unpredictably without one initially noticing and are thus essentially rendered almost meaningless. However, the most dangerous property of the level is undeniably some parts of the sand itself; sometimes, one may rarely happen to traverse across an area of sand that sucks in anyone above it, as well as anything they may be carrying on their person. Wanderers who end up in this sand will suddenly and rapidly begin sinking below its surface; within about a minute, their entire body will be trapped underneath the sand, where they presumably suffocate to death. Unfortunately, it is apparently impossible to tell where these areas of 'quicksand' are due to them appearing identical to the rest of the sand in '''Level 135''' and somehow not sucking in anything other than wanderers. As a result, guesswork is often heavily relied upon for survival when traversing the level. ==Entities== No entity has ever been encountered in '''Level 135''', and it is believed that there are either no entities in the level or the isolation effect that applies to wanderers in the level also applies to entities in said level. Despite the apparent absence of any entities, there have been five recorded accounts from wanderers pertaining to a mythical entity that they referred to as "The One Beneath", with these accounts often not making sense at all and indicating that some of the aforementioned wanderers have become mentally disturbed in some way. All of these wanderers had previously been in '''Level 135''' before becoming unwaveringly convinced that The One Beneath exists, leading some researchers to believe that this supposedly irrational belief is somehow caused by the level itself. Supposedly, the entity resides somewhere in '''Level 135'''. One account — a note left by one of the affected wanderers — is provided below.

Recovered Note:

''The One Beneath''

Whatever you think you know about '''Level 135''', there is more to it. At first, I had no idea of what lurks below the surface. I was just a blissfully oblivious wanderer, trying to find a safe haven. And then I arrived at '''Level 135'''. I was cursed with the truth of what lies below. '''Level 135''' is inhabited by a powerful entity called The One Beneath. It lives below the level's surface. Those who sink beneath the sands don't just die of suffocation; they become its victims. Many are lost to The One Beneath as time passes. It's always waiting, ready to claim new victims. Just don't go to the level — it's not worth the risk. You don't know how many wanderers it has taken. Its kingdom is the darkness below the sands. It's The One Beneath.
==Colonies & Outposts== Because of the unpredictable nature of '''Level 135''' and its isolating geometry, no known colonies or outposts have been established in the level and creating any is impossible. ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== *Falling asleep on the sandbar of [[Level 100]] has a small chance to send one here. *Writing "135-131" in the sand at the edge of [[Level 131]] will lead here. ===Exits=== *Noclipping through the sand has a high chance of sending one to a random beach-related [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Aquatic aquatic] level, though there is a small chance that one will end up in another random level instead. *Randomly no-clipping will take one to [[Level 136]]. *One may rarely come across an apparently endless body of water, heralded by a slight decline in the sand's surface height and the presence of small waves; venturing out into this body of water will lead one to the sea of [[Level 468.1]]. ---- {{Collapse|num=1|initial= '''Open Author & Credits Info''' |final= '''Close Author & Credits Info''' |body= '''Author:''' [[User:Forknife468152|Forknife]] '''Body Background Image Code:''' From [[User:Cringeaccount123456789|Cringeaccount]]'s anomalous level: [[Safe at Last]] with minor modifications by me {{User:Forknife468152/Pages}} }} {{Redirect|Prev=134|Next=136}}{{VoidedTheme}} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Dangerous]] [[zh:Level 135]]
